title: Immutable Audit Log

story.i_want_to: have every mutation recorded in an immutable, append-only audit log with full before/after state snapshots
story.so_that: there is a tamper-proof record of who changed what and when, enabling forensic investigation and compliance auditing

rationale: In an agent-driven system where AI agents self-report progress, an immutable audit trail is critical for trust and accountability. Without it, there is no way to reconstruct what actually happened or detect discrepancies between claimed and actual work. The audit log also serves as the foundation for the change feed (US-3.2) and story history (US-3.3) features.

acceptance_criteria.description: An audit_log table exists with columns: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK, nullable for superadmin), entity_type (string), entity_id (uuid), action (string), actor_type (string), actor_id (uuid), actor_label (string), old_state (jsonb), new_state (jsonb), metadata (jsonb), inserted_at (utc_datetime). No updated_at column.
The audit_log table has NO UPDATE or DELETE RLS policies — only INSERT and SELECT. A database-level trigger or rule prevents UPDATE and DELETE operations on the table entirely.
An Audit context module (Loopctl.Audit) provides a create_log_entry/1 function that inserts a new audit log entry. No update or delete functions exist in the module.

Every mutation across tenant-scoped entities (projects, epics, stories, agents, artifact_reports, verification_results, orchestrator_state, webhooks, api_keys) records an audit log entry in the same Ecto.Multi transaction as the mutation, capturing entity_type, entity_id, action, actor details, and old_state/new_state.
GET /api/v1/audit returns paginated audit log entries for the authenticated tenant, supporting filters: entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_type, actor_id, date range (from/to as ISO8601).
The audit endpoint requires role user or higher (user, orchestrator with read). Agent role keys cannot access the audit endpoint directly.
Audit log entries are tenant-scoped via RLS. A tenant can only read their own audit log entries. Entries with tenant_id=NULL are only visible to superadmin.
The old_state field captures the entity's state before the mutation (NULL for create actions). The new_state field captures the entity's state after the mutation (NULL for delete actions).
Audit log entries include a composite index on (tenant_id, entity_type, entity_id) and an index on (tenant_id, inserted_at) for efficient querying by entity and date range.
The Ecto schema for audit_log has no changeset function that allows updates. The schema module only exposes a create_changeset/1 function.

test_cases.name: audit log entry created on story status change
expected_results: Audit log entry exists with action='status_changed'
old_state contains agent_status='pending'
new_state contains agent_status='assigned'
actor_type is 'api_key'
actor_id matches the API key ID
tenant_id matches the tenant

test_cases.name: audit log is append-only — updates rejected
steps: Attempt to update the audit log entry via Ecto.Repo.update
Attempt to delete the audit log entry via Ecto.Repo.delete
expected_results: Update operation raises an error or is rejected by database rule
Delete operation raises an error or is rejected by database rule
Original entry remains unchanged in the database

test_cases.name: GET /audit returns filtered audit entries
preconditions: Multiple audit log entries with different entity_types and actions
expected_results: Response returns only entries matching entity_type=story AND action=status_changed
Date range filter returns only entries within the specified range
entity_id filter returns only entries for that specific entity
All responses return 200 with JSON array of audit entries

test_cases.name: tenant isolation — cannot read other tenant's audit log
preconditions: Audit log entries exist for both tenants
steps: GET /api/v1/audit with Tenant A's API key
GET /api/v1/audit with Tenant B's API key
expected_results: Tenant A sees only Tenant A's audit entries
Tenant B sees only Tenant B's audit entries
Neither tenant sees the other's entries

test_cases.name: audit log captures old_state and new_state correctly
steps: PATCH /api/v1/projects/:id with {name: 'Updated Name'} using user API key
Query audit_log for entity_type='project', entity_id=project.id, action='updated'
expected_results: old_state includes name='Original Name'
new_state includes name='Updated Name'
Both old_state and new_state are valid JSON objects

test_cases.name: audit log entry created for entity creation with null old_state
steps: POST /api/v1/projects with valid project data using user API key
expected_results: Audit log entry exists with action='created'
old_state is NULL
new_state contains the created project's data
inserted_at is set to current time

test_cases.name: agent role cannot access audit endpoint
expected_results: Response is 403 Forbidden
Error message indicates insufficient permissions

test_cases.name: audit log pagination
preconditions: 25 audit log entries exist for the tenant
expected_results: Page 1 returns 10 entries
Page 2 returns 10 entries
Page 3 returns 5 entries
Entries are ordered by inserted_at descending (most recent first)
Response includes pagination metadata (total, page, page_size)

technical_notes: Create migration with audit_log table — no updated_at column. Add database-level rules (CREATE RULE or trigger) to prevent UPDATE and DELETE.
RLS policies: INSERT allowed for all authenticated roles, SELECT restricted by tenant_id. No UPDATE or DELETE policies.
Add composite index on (tenant_id, entity_type, entity_id) and index on (tenant_id, inserted_at) for query performance.
Loopctl.Audit context module: create_log_entry/1, list_entries/2 (with filters), get_entry!/1. No update/delete functions.
AuditLog schema: create_changeset/1 only. No update changeset.
Helper function Loopctl.Audit.log_in_multi/3 to add an audit log insert step to any Ecto.Multi pipeline.
Controller: AuditController with index action. Filters parsed from query params: entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_type, actor_id, from, to.
Route: GET /api/v1/audit, guarded by RequireRole plug for :user role minimum.
